preamble: The initial devoicing of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] to [s] is a significant driver of Tonogenesis through register splitting. Syllables that historically began with [z] shifted to a lower pitch register, as seen across various Sinitic and Tai language families.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, Register Split, Tonogenesis, Voice Loss

examples.note: Initial voicing in fricatives led to the development of the Yáng (low) register tones.
Led to the split of tones into high and low registers based on the voicing of the original initial.

sources: Haudricourt, A. G. (1954). De l'origine des tons en viêtnamien.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
